title: Glorious Smoky Malaysian Prawn Pilaf
description: A flavorful and aromatic Malaysian dish featuring prawns, parma ham, and a blend of spices, perfect for a lazy Sunday lunch. This pescatarian-friendly recipe is cooked to perfection on a salt block.
instructions: Preheat the salt block in the oven at 400 degrees Fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Heat 2 tablespoons of oil on the preheated salt block over medium heat.
Add the diced parma ham and cook until crispy, then set aside.
Add the prawns to the salt block and cook until they turn pink, about 2-3 minutes per side.
Add the smoked paprika, malaysian curry powder, salt, and black pepper to the salt block and stir for 1 minute.
Add the basmati rice to the salt block and stir to combine with the spices and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the coconut milk to the salt block and bring to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the salt block with a lid, and cook for 15 minutes or until the rice is cooked and the liquid is absorbed.
Stir in the shredded mozzarella cheese until melted and well combined.
Garnish with chopped scallions and crispy parma ham.
